prompt: {{ mode==0 ? 'A risk threatens the transition out of Planning. Tap the milestone that is the sponsor decision gate the project must pass before Execution can officially begin.' : (mode==1 ? 'Tap the milestone after which any further change to project scope must be handled through formal change control.' : (mode==2 ? 'Tap the milestone at which the project is formally authorized and the project manager gains the authority to apply resources.' : (mode==3 ? 'Tap the milestone that marks the release of the product to operations — the hand-off out of the project.' : 'Tap the milestone that serves as both the phase gate before Execution and the trigger point for releasing contingency reserves.'))) }}

value: the project kickoff — work starts, but it is not a formal authority gate
the charter approval — the project is formally authorized and the PM gains authority to apply resources here
the scope baseline — after this point, scope changes must go through formal change control
the sponsor go/no-go gate before Execution, and the point contingency reserves may be released
go-live — the product is released to operations

items.label: fullNames[i]
items.why: i == target ? ('Correct — ' + fullNames[i] + ' is ' + roleText[i] + '.') : (fullNames[i] + ' is ' + roleText[i] + ' — not what this question asks.')

explanation: {{fullNames[target]}} is {{roleText[target]}}. Milestones are zero-duration control points — identifying each one's role (authorization, scope baseline, phase gate, external sign-off, release) is the skill being tested.

prompt: Tap every milestone that is a formal EXTERNAL approval or stakeholder sign-off — a decision made by a party outside the project team, not an internal checkpoint.

value: an approval by the external regulator
a sign-off by the external client

items.label: fullNames[i]
items.why: (i == 4 || i == 5) ? (fullNames[i] + ' — ' + roleText[i] + '. An external party decides it, so it counts.') : (fullNames[i] + ' is ' + roleText[i] + ' — an internal control point, not an external sign-off.')

explanation: External approvals come from parties outside the project team — here the regulator (Regulatory approval) and the client (Client sign-off). Kickoff, charter approval, the scope baseline, the sponsor gate and go-live are all internal governance points.

prompt: The bands show the project phases along the timeline. Drag the sponsor-approval milestone (◆) to the gate the project must clear before it can move from finalising the schedule and design into hands-on building and delivery of the product.
caption: A milestone is a zero-duration marker placed on a date.

bars.label: 'Gate'
bars.why: 'The sponsor go/no-go gate sits where Planning ends and Execution begins — the end of week 4.'

explanation: The sponsor go/no-go gate sits at the Planning→Execution boundary: Execution cannot start until the sponsor approves at the end of Planning (week 4). A milestone is a zero-duration marker on a single date, not a span of work.
